name: Minimum Preflight CLI (PR-F3, handover F-011)

design_notes.nature_of_this_feature: Handover F-011. This is the phase's FIRST executable-code feature (a Python CLI), following ten prose/schema/JSON-registration features (031-040). Per feature_list_005.json feature 041 acceptance core: (1) 'CLI emits schema-valid preflight and baseline artifacts'; (2) 'CLI supports framework-self fixture mode'; (3) 'Failure exit codes are documented'; plus the four literal acceptance tests (py_compile-valid; framework-self fixture run schema-validating against both shipped schemas; exit codes documented in --help or module docstring; validate.sh stays green). The CLI implements the MINIMUM viable slice of ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md (the three-verdict vocabulary, the blocking-vs-non-blocking distinction, the operator-exception rule) and ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md (the six baseline field categories, each item revision+timestamp anchored) -- NOT the full protocol surface. Deliberately out of scope for this minimum CLI: consumer-registered custom blocking conditions, P0-defect/H-RISK scanning (no audit-findings source exists yet -- schema/engineering_finding.schema.json ships in 039 but no .agent/audits/*/findings.json producer exists until feature 044), and any typed cross-repository dependency reconciliation (ecosystem/04_dependency_reconciliation.md is Planned, not shipped). Deferred, richer preflight behavior is future features' scope (raised as a documented, honest limitation below, not silently glossed over).
design_notes.cli_interface: tools/ecosystem_preflight.py, a single-file, Python-3-standard-library-only script (argparse, json, os, platform, subprocess, sys, datetime -- no new runtime dependency; the repository has no prior Python file, so this establishes house Python style: module docstring with an exit-code table, argparse with a custom error()-override so malformed invocations exit 64 rather than argparse's default 2, small pure functions, explicit error handling, no bare except). CLI flags: --execution-id (required) the ecosystem-cycle execution identifier; --output-dir (required) where preflight.json / baseline.json / a collection-log.txt evidence file are written (created if absent) -- deliberately NEVER defaults to .agent/reconciliation/<id>/ in this feature, so this feature's own verification writes only to caller-supplied scratch directories and never touches the real .agent/ tree (the actual .agent/reconciliation/<id>/ real-artifact write is feature 043's dogfood-run scope, per product_spec_005 Sec 9's 'Required first-run outputs (feature 043)' wording -- this feature ships the tool, 043 runs it for real); --repo-root (default '.') the git working tree to inspect; --self-fixture (boolean) resolves --repo-root, when not explicitly overridden, via `git -C <script's own directory> rev-parse --show-toplevel` -- i.e. it auto-detects the repository the shipped script itself lives inside, enabling framework dogfooding (and later, feature 043's real run) without the caller needing to know the absolute path; --tolerate-untracked PATH (repeatable) repo-relative paths of untracked files the operator has explicitly declared tolerated, per protocol Sec 4's 'has not explicitly declared tolerated' language; --operator-approver / --operator-authorization / --operator-approved-at (optional trio) the structured operator-approval decision the Operator-Exception Rule (protocol Sec 5) requires before a PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS run may proceed -- this deterministic CLI has no interactive prompt, so the approval decision is supplied as explicit, auditable CLI flags representing a decision the operator already made out-of-band.
design_notes.deterministic_collection_design: Two blocking-rule checks, directly implementing the MINIMUM slice of protocol Sec 4 that is implementable today without additional not-yet-shipped infrastructure: (1) git clean-state -- `git status --porcelain=v1` against --repo-root; any line NOT starting with '??' (i.e. any staged or unstaged change to a TRACKED file) is unconditionally blocking; any '??' (untracked) line whose path is NOT in the --tolerate-untracked set is blocking (protocol Sec 4 bullet 1, 'uncommitted or untracked changes ... not explicitly declared tolerated'); any '??' line whose path IS declared tolerated is surfaced as a non-blocking finding (an 'exception'), never silently dropped -- this is the mechanism that produces a genuine PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS verdict. (2) required-reference resolution -- schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json and schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json (the two schemas this CLI's own output must validate against) must resolve on disk under --repo-root, or each missing path is its own blocking finding (protocol Sec 4 bullet 2, 'a required ... reference ... is missing, unreadable, or does not resolve to an existing path'). A blocking finding is NEVER downgraded to a non-blocking exception by the tool itself (protocol Sec 4's non-downgrade invariant) -- proven by verification entry 15 (blocking-over-exceptions precedence). Consumer-registered custom blocking conditions (Sec 4 bullet 4) and the P0/H-RISK gate (Sec 4 bullet 3) are explicitly NOT implemented by this minimum CLI (see non_goals) -- there is no typed source of either in the framework yet.
design_notes.verdict_and_exit_code_design: Exit codes (documented verbatim in the module docstring AND surfaced via --help, satisfying the literal acceptance test through either channel): 0 = PASS (no blocking finding, no exception; both preflight.json and baseline.json are written, schema-valid). 1 = FAIL (at least one blocking finding; ONLY preflight.json is written -- protocol Sec 2 forbids capturing a baseline against FAIL, so baseline.json is never written on this path; verification entry 7 proves this). 2 = PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS, PENDING (no blocking finding, but one or more declared-tolerated-untracked exceptions were surfaced, and no --operator-approver/--operator-authorization were supplied) -- per protocol Sec 5 ('MUST halt ... MUST NOT proceed ... until an operator has reviewed'), the tool WITHHOLDS the schema-gated preflight.json entirely (writing only an informational, deliberately non-schema-conformant preflight.pending.json so the pending exceptions are still visible to the operator) and writes no baseline.json -- verification entry 10 proves the pending file, if run through jsonschema.validate against schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, is REJECTED (missing operator_approval), so the tool structurally cannot smuggle an unapproved PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS verdict as a schema-valid artifact. 3 = PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS, APPROVED (same exceptions, but --operator-approver and --operator-authorization were supplied) -- preflight.json is written with a populated operator_approval block (approver / approved_at [defaults to generated_at if not given] / authorization, matching schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json's conditional 'if verdict==PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS then require operator_approval+exceptions' rule exactly) and baseline.json is captured (protocol Sec 2: baseline capture follows PASS or an APPROVED PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS). 64 = usage error (missing/invalid CLI arguments -- argparse's error() is overridden to raise a custom exception the CLI catches and maps to exit 64, distinct from every domain exit code 0-3).
design_notes.baseline_field_synthesis: schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json requires all SIX field categories present with >=1 anchored (revision+timestamp) item each. This minimum CLI derives all six deterministically from local, inspectable state, never fabricating an unlabelled value: framework_references and repository_references both anchor to --repo-root's own `git rev-parse HEAD` (the minimum-viable default: in self-fixture/single-repo mode the framework IS the repository being inspected; richer multi-framework-pin baselines are later features' scope, stated as a non-goal below); dependency_references anchors to the CLI's own Python runtime version (`platform.python_version()`) as the one real, inspectable tooling dependency this deterministic collection itself relies on; ci_references anchors to repo HEAD with an explicit, HONEST additionalProperties 'note' field stating no externally-verified CI run is available in this offline/self-fixture mode (a documented KNOWN LIMITATION, not a silent fabrication of a fake CI run -- flagged for a future NDEBT registration, out of this contract's own scope per non_goals); planning_references anchors to docs/planning/manifest.json's path at repo HEAD; evidence_references anchors to the CLI's own emitted collection-log.txt evidence file under --output-dir. captured_at/generated_at are read once per run via a single UTC clock read (methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md's Clock-Read Timestamps rule), consistent across every field emitted in that run.
design_notes.dry_run_proof: The full candidate verification suite (22 entries, matching this contract's 'verification' array 1:1 in order) was executed against a scratch prototype implementing the design above, in two configurations, per protocol: (A) a fully disposable `cp -a` clone of the real nizam-framework repository (`.git` included) at the current HEAD 17408d942cb09acced7c0874cd4bd363dce3bfc5, with the prototype copied in as tools/ecosystem_preflight.py (never committed) -- ALL 22 entries PASS, including: py_compile success; the HEAD-anchored anti-vacuity check (tools/ecosystem_preflight.py absent from HEAD); the literal exit-code-documentation AT (via --help grep, independently also satisfied via the docstring path); a structural exit-code-table regex check (5 rows: 0/PASS, 1/FAIL, 2/PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS-pending, 3/PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS-approved, 64/usage error); a --self-fixture run against the disposable clone's OWN tree (auto-resolving repo-root via `git -C tools rev-parse --show-toplevel` from inside the clone, exactly as it will when the real file ships at tools/ecosystem_preflight.py), whose preflight.json and baseline.json both independently validate against the real, unmodified schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json and schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json; output isolation (git status before/after an entire self-fixture run is byte-identical -- the tool writes NOTHING into the repository tree, only into --output-dir); six purpose-built disposable-git-repo fixtures (constructed live inside each verification command via `mktemp -d` + `git init`, never committed to any repository, matching the house 'disposable clone' precedent rather than a nested-.git static fixture) proving: a dirty-tracked-file FAIL (exit 1, no baseline.json), an untracked-not-declared-tolerated FAIL (exit 1, default-deny), a missing-required-reference FAIL (exit 1, both missing schema paths individually named in blocking_findings), a PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS-pending run (exit 2, preflight.json absent, preflight.pending.json present and schema-REJECTED), a PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS-approved run (exit 3, both artifacts schema-valid, operator_approval fields exactly matching the supplied CLI flags), and a clean-tree PASS run (exit 0, both artifacts schema-valid, no exceptions/operator_approval keys present); two-run repeatability (same unchanged fixture, two execution-ids, identical PASS verdict both times); the usage-error path (missing --execution-id, exit exactly 64, distinct from 0/1/2/3); a blocking-precedence probe (a fixture with BOTH a dirty tracked file AND a tolerated-untracked file AND operator-approval flags supplied simultaneously still returns FAIL/exit 1 -- blocking never silently loses to exceptions); `bash tools/validate.sh` SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed on the disposable clone with the new file present; and all five non-goal/scope-guard/immutability checks (17-22) green. (B) the SAME 22-entry suite run against the REAL, current, unimplemented nizam-framework working tree (tools/ecosystem_preflight.py genuinely absent) -- entries 1, 3, 4, 5, 7 through 15 correctly and legitimately FAIL for file-absence reasons (proving these checks are not vacuously green before the deliverable exists); entries 2 (HEAD-anchored anti-vacuity), 16 (validator green), and 17-22 (all non-goal/immutability/scope-guard checks) correctly PASS on the real, unimplemented tree, exactly as expected pre-implementation. Nothing under the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework was created, edited, or deleted to produce any of the above (confirmed via `git status --porcelain` before and after every probe); every proof ran against the disposable full clone or ephemeral `mktemp -d` fixture repos outside the repository tree.
design_notes.run_state_and_immutable_artifacts: This is a CONTRACT-PROPOSAL step (status=proposed): no implementation edit, no git commit, and no .agent/run_state.json rewrite happens under this document -- run_state.json is orchestrator-owned and batched centrally. Nothing under tools/, schema/, ecosystem/, NIZAM.json, or any other repo-tree surface was created, edited, or deleted to produce this proposal or its dry-run proofs; every proof ran against a disposable full clone or ephemeral fixture repos under the session scratchpad directory, never the real repository tree.
design_notes.revision_1_content_honesty_fix: REVISION 1 of up to 3 (per .agent/qa/041-contract-review.json, revision_reviewed=0, REJECTED, single HIGH finding, non-blocking observations otherwise). Gap: entries 5, 11, and 12 (round 0) validated baseline.json's SHAPE against schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json but never its TRUTH -- the evaluator built mutants/mutant_fabricated.py (honest blocking/exception/exit-code mechanics, but a fabricated, unrelated 40-hex SHA in place of the fixture's real `git rev-parse HEAD`, a stale hardcoded 2020-01-01 timestamp, and a fabricated 'run_id'+'status':'verified-green' claim in ci_references instead of the promised honest limitation note) and proved it passes all 22 entries unmodified, directly contradicting design_notes.baseline_field_synthesis's real-HEAD-anchoring and honest-ci-note claims. FIX in this revision (narrow, exactly as required): entries 5, 11, and 12 (the only three entries that ever inspect baseline.json) are each extended with three additional, independently-recomputed content-honesty assertions, layered after their existing schema-validation step: (a) `repository_references[0]['revision']` and `framework_references[0]['revision']` must equal a `git rev-parse HEAD` (or `git -C <fixture> rev-parse HEAD`) recomputed FRESH inside the same verification command, not merely be a non-empty string; (b) `ci_references[0]` must carry a genuine, non-empty 'note' key that reads as an honest CI-limitation statement (mentions 'ci' plus one of 'limitation'/'no'/'not'), and must NOT carry a 'run_id' key or a 'status'=='verified-green' value; (c) `captured_at` must parse as ISO-8601 and fall within 600 seconds of the verification command's own wall-clock read, catching a stale/unrelated timestamp. Re-proven on the disposable full clone (HEAD 17408d942cb09acced7c0874cd4bd363dce3bfc5) in BOTH directions for all three revised entries: the honest prototype (unchanged from round 0) PASSES entries 5, 11, and 12 with the new assertions active; a freshly rebuilt scratch mutant reproducing the evaluator's exact fabrication shape (mutants/mutant_fabricated.py: hardcoded 'cafef00d...' SHA, hardcoded '2020-01-01T00:00:00Z' timestamp, fabricated run_id/verified-green ci_references) FAILS all three revised entries -- entry 5's AssertionError names the exact SHA mismatch ('cafef00d...' vs the clone's real HEAD c9bb8b81f...); entries 11 and 12 fail identically against a disposable-fixture-rooted variant of the same mutant, each independently recomputing that fixture's own real HEAD via `git -C "$T" rev-parse HEAD` (not the outer clone's HEAD) and catching the same fabricated SHA. All 22 entries were re-run against the honest prototype after this revision (22/22 PASS, unchanged from round 0 for the other 19 entries); nothing else in the contract (scope, non_goals, other 19 verification entries, estimated_lines) was altered by this revision. Nothing under the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework was touched to produce this fix or its proofs -- all mutant construction and both-direction re-testing ran against the same disposable `cp -a` clone and ephemeral `mktemp -d` fixtures used in round 0.

files_create.purpose: The minimum deterministic preflight CLI: implements the minimum viable slice of ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md's three-verdict/blocking/operator-exception mechanics and ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md's six-category/revision-anchored baseline shape; emits schema-valid preflight.json (always) and baseline.json (on PASS or approved PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS only) under a caller-supplied --output-dir; supports --self-fixture framework dogfooding; documents its five exit codes (0/1/2/3/64) in its module docstring and --help output.

non_goals: No capability registration of tools/ecosystem_preflight.py in NIZAM.json's capabilities array, tools/skill.json's capabilities array, or tools/SKILL.md's routing section -- feature 041's own acceptance tests (feature_list_005.json) require none of this, and 040's own non_goals already deferred 'any other deterministic ecosystem tooling' registration explicitly to this feature's future or a later feature; capability routing for the CLI is left to a later feature's explicit scope.

No implementation of ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md Sec 4 bullet 3 (the P0-defect / H-RISK acceptance-of-risk gate) -- no typed source of unresolved audit findings exists yet (schema/engineering_finding.schema.json ships in 039, but no .agent/audits/*/findings.json producer exists until feature 044); implementing this gate against a non-existent evidence source would be unfounded, not minimum-viable.

No implementation of ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md Sec 4 bullet 4 (consumer-registered, repository-specific custom blocking conditions) -- no consumer preflight-configuration schema or file exists yet; deferred to a future feature if a consumer ever needs it.

No implementation of ecosystem/04_dependency_reconciliation.md (typed cross-repository dependency verification) -- that protocol document is Planned, not shipped, and does not exist in this repository; dependency_references in the emitted baseline is deliberately the CLI's own Python-runtime-version self-reference, not a resolved typed cross-repo dependency graph.

No default write path under .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/ or .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/ -- --output-dir is a required, explicit CLI argument in this feature with no default resolving into .agent/; the real per-protocol .agent/reconciliation/<id>/ write, against the real nizam-framework repository, is feature 043's (Dogfood Framework Preflight) scope, which depends on 042 (validator + CI fixture extension), not this feature.

No resolution of a real CI run for ci_references -- the emitted baseline's CI reference is repo-HEAD-anchored with an explicit, honest 'note' documenting this known limitation; wiring an actual CI-run lookup (e.g. a GitHub Actions API call) is out of this minimum CLI's scope and is not required by any of feature 041's acceptance tests.

No interactive operator prompt -- the Operator-Exception Rule (protocol Sec 5) is satisfied via explicit --operator-approver/--operator-authorization/--operator-approved-at CLI flags representing an out-of-band decision already made by the operator, not an interactive terminal prompt.

No edit to ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md, ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md, or any other ecosystem/*.md protocol document -- protocol prose is 033/034's already-shipped scope, immutable here.

No edit to schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, or any other schema/*.json file -- 037/038's already-shipped scope; this feature's CLI output must conform to those schemas exactly as shipped, never adjust them to fit.

No edit to NIZAM.json, tools/skill.json, or tools/SKILL.md -- 040's already-shipped scope (capability routing for the four shipped protocols); this feature's own capability registration is explicitly deferred (see the first non-goal above).

No edit to tools/validate.sh, tools/verify_lib.sh, tools/interface.md, tools/README.md, tools/SKILL.md, tools/skill.json, tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh, or any existing tools/fixtures/*.json fixture -- extending the validator with new checks or fixtures for the ecosystem schema families is feature 042's (Extend Validator + CI Fixtures) explicit scope, not this one's.

No creation of any file under tools/fixtures/ for this feature -- unlike the static JSON fixtures 037/038/039 shipped, this CLI's behavioral fixtures (clean/dirty/missing-reference/pending/approved disposable git repos) are constructed ephemerally inside each verification command via mktemp -d + git init, never committed to the repository (nested .git directories inside the framework's own repository would be a git-submodule-like hazard and are avoided entirely, mirroring the disposable-full-clone precedent already established by contracts 032-040's own dry-run proofs).

No edit to docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, or docs/planning/DEBT.md -- registering the ci_references known-limitation as its own NDEBT-### entry (if warranted) is a later orchestrator/evaluator action, not this contract's own scope.
No edit to CHANGELOG.md -- CHANGELOG consolidation for phase 005 is feature 045's (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE) responsibility, per the 037/038/039/040 precedent.
No edit to .agent/run_state.json -- orchestrator-owned, batched centrally, not gated by any verification entry below (038/039/040 precedent).

No creation, edit, or deletion of the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md -- tolerated in the scope-guard allow-list solely to avoid a false-positive scope-guard failure, not because this feature is authorized to modify it.

No edit to any .agent/contracts/*.json other than this 041.json, or any .agent/qa/*.json -- those are immutable audit records or owned by the orchestrator/evaluator; other concurrently in-flight phase-005 contract/qa artifacts are tolerated as untracked additions per the two-part immutability guard, never asserted byte-identical.
No git commit of any kind is performed under this contract or its implementation step -- the deliverable is verified in the working tree; committing is a separate, later pipeline action.

No execution of any outstanding phase-005 human gate (H-FRAMEWORK-SCOPE, H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE, H-RISK) -- this feature's operator_gate is false; the --operator-approver/--operator-authorization CLI flags this tool accepts are the deterministic-tool-side mechanics of protocol Sec 5's Operator-Exception Rule, not an execution of any of these four named human gates.

verification.acceptance_test: F-011/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): the CLI exists and is syntactically valid Python.
verification.command: test -f tools/ecosystem_preflight.py && python3 -m py_compile tools/ecosystem_preflight.py && echo PASS || { echo "tools/ecosystem_preflight.py missing or fails to compile"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY (HEAD-anchored): tools/ecosystem_preflight.py does not exist at HEAD, proving this is a genuinely new file, not an edit to a pre-existing one.
verification.command: git cat-file -e HEAD:tools/ecosystem_preflight.py 2>/dev/null && { echo "tools/ecosystem_preflight.py already exists at HEAD -- would be vacuous"; exit 1; } || echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-011/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): failure exit codes are documented in the CLI (help text or module docstring).
verification.command: ( python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --help 2>&1 | grep -Eqi 'exit|code|fail' || python3 -c "import ast,sys; t=ast.get_docstring(ast.parse(open('tools/ecosystem_preflight.py').read())); sys.exit(0 if t and ('exit' in t.lower()) else 1)" ) && echo PASS || { echo "exit codes are not documented via --help or the module docstring"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: STRUCTURAL (defense-in-depth beyond the loose literal AT above): the module docstring documents a genuine exit-code table naming all five distinct codes (0=PASS, 1=FAIL, 2=PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS pending, 3=PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS approved, 64=usage error), not merely the word 'exit' once.
verification.command:
python3 -c "import ast,re,sys
t = ast.get_docstring(ast.parse(open('tools/ecosystem_preflight.py').read())) or ''
required = [
    (r'^\s*0\b.*PASS\b', '0 -> PASS'),
    (r'^\s*1\b.*FAIL\b', '1 -> FAIL'),
    (r'^\s*2\b.*PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS\b', '2 -> PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS (pending)'),
    (r'^\s*3\b.*PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS\b', '3 -> PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS (approved)'),
    (r'^\s*64\b.*[Uu]sage error\b', '64 -> usage error'),
]
lines = t.splitlines()
missing = [label for pat, label in required if not any(re.search(pat, ln) for ln in lines)]
if missing:
    print('missing exit-code table rows:', missing); sys.exit(1)
print('exit-code table structurally present')
" && echo PASS || { echo "exit-code table is not structurally present in the module docstring"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-011/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json) + CONTENT HONESTY (per .agent/qa/041-contract-review.json HIGH finding): running in framework-self fixture mode emits a preflight artifact validating against schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json and a baseline artifact validating against schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, against the REAL current tree (robust to whatever it currently has untracked); AND the baseline's repository_references/framework_references revision values are independently recomputed and asserted to equal the real `git rev-parse HEAD`, its captured_at timestamp is asserted fresh (within 600s of the actual run clock), and its ci_references item is asserted to carry a genuine, non-empty honest limitation note and NOT a fabricated run_id/verified-green claim -- so a CLI that fabricates baseline content while implementing the verdict mechanics honestly is caught, not merely schema-shape-validated.
verification.command:
OUT=$(mktemp -d); UNTRACKED=$(git status --porcelain | awk '/^\?\? / {print $2}'); ARGS=(); for f in $UNTRACKED; do ARGS+=(--tolerate-untracked "$f"); done; REAL_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD); python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id verify-041-selffixture --output-dir "$OUT" --self-fixture "${ARGS[@]}" --operator-approver "contract-041-verification" --operator-authorization "verification run for feature 041"; rc=$?; if [ "$rc" -ne 0 ] && [ "$rc" -ne 3 ]; then echo "unexpected exit $rc"; exit 1; fi; python3 -c "
import json, jsonschema, datetime
pv = json.load(open('schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json'))
bv = json.load(open('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json'))
jsonschema.validate(json.load(open('$OUT/preflight.json')), pv)
b = json.load(open('$OUT/baseline.json'))
jsonschema.validate(b, bv)
real_head = '$REAL_HEAD'
assert b['repository_references'][0]['revision'] == real_head, ('repository_references revision mismatch', b['repository_references'][0]['revision'], real_head)
assert b['framework_references'][0]['revision'] == real_head, ('framework_references revision mismatch', b['framework_references'][0]['revision'], real_head)
ci = b['ci_references'][0]
note = ci.get('note', '')
assert isinstance(note, str) and note.strip(), ('ci_references missing a non-empty honest limitation note', ci)
assert 'ci' in note.lower() and ('limitation' in note.lower() or 'no' in note.lower() or 'not' in note.lower()), ('ci_references note does not read as an honest limitation statement', ci)
assert 'run_id' not in ci, ('ci_references fabricates a run_id as if a real CI run were checked', ci)
assert ci.get('status') != 'verified-green', ('ci_references fabricates a verified-green claim', ci)
ts = b['captured_at'].rstrip('Z')
parsed = datetime.datetime.fromisoformat(ts).replace(tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)
now = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc)
delta = abs((now - parsed).total_seconds())
assert delta < 600, ('captured_at is stale or not close to the actual run clock', b['captured_at'], delta)
print('self-fixture artifacts schema-valid AND content-anchored (real HEAD, honest CI note, fresh timestamp)')
" && echo PASS || { echo "self-fixture run failed content-honesty checks (HEAD anchor / honest CI note / fresh timestamp)"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: OUTPUT ISOLATION (code-quality surface specific to executable tooling): a full --self-fixture run against the real repository writes nothing into the repository tree itself -- only into the caller-supplied --output-dir.
verification.command: BEFORE=$(git status --porcelain | sort); OUT=$(mktemp -d); UNTRACKED=$(git status --porcelain | awk '/^\?\? / {print $2}'); ARGS=(); for f in $UNTRACKED; do ARGS+=(--tolerate-untracked "$f"); done; python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id verify-041-isolation --output-dir "$OUT" --self-fixture "${ARGS[@]}" >/dev/null; rc=$?; [ "$rc" -eq 64 ] && { echo "usage error (rc=64) -- CLI invocation itself is broken"; exit 1; }; AFTER=$(git status --porcelain | sort); [ "$BEFORE" = "$AFTER" ] && echo PASS || { echo "CLI run altered the repository tree's git status"; echo "BEFORE=[$BEFORE]"; echo "AFTER=[$AFTER]"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: UNIT/BEHAVIOR FIXTURE (FAIL, dirty tracked file): a disposable git fixture with an uncommitted change to a tracked file yields exit 1, a schema-valid FAIL verdict, and NO baseline.json (protocol Sec 2: baseline never captured against FAIL).
verification.command:
T=$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p "$T/schema"; cp schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json "$T/schema/"; ( cd "$T" && git init -q && git config user.email a@a.com && git config user.name a && git add -A && git commit -q -m init ); echo '// dirty' >> "$T/schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json"; OUT=$(mktemp -d); python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id verify-fail --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$T"; rc=$?; [ "$rc" -eq 1 ] || { echo "expected exit 1, got $rc"; exit 1; }; test -f "$OUT/baseline.json" && { echo "baseline must not be captured on FAIL"; exit 1; }; python3 -c "import json,jsonschema
pv=json.load(open('schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json'))
d=json.load(open('$OUT/preflight.json'))
jsonschema.validate(d, pv)
assert d['verdict']=='FAIL', d
" && echo PASS || { echo "FAIL fixture did not behave as contracted"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: UNIT/BEHAVIOR FIXTURE (FAIL, default-deny): a disposable git fixture with an untracked file NOT declared tolerated is blocking (exit 1), never silently downgraded to a non-blocking exception (protocol Sec 4's non-downgrade invariant).
verification.command: T=$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p "$T/schema"; cp schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json "$T/schema/"; ( cd "$T" && git init -q && git config user.email a@a.com && git config user.name a && git add -A && git commit -q -m init ); echo x > "$T/EXTRA.md"; OUT=$(mktemp -d); python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id verify-untracked --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$T"; rc=$?; [ "$rc" -eq 1 ] && echo PASS || { echo "expected exit 1 (default-deny untracked), got $rc"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: UNIT/BEHAVIOR FIXTURE (FAIL, missing required reference): a disposable git fixture lacking schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json and schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json yields exit 1 with both missing paths individually named in the verdict's blocking_findings.
verification.command:
T=$(mktemp -d); echo x > "$T/README.md"; ( cd "$T" && git init -q && git config user.email a@a.com && git config user.name a && git add -A && git commit -q -m init ); OUT=$(mktemp -d); python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id verify-missing-ref --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$T"; rc=$?; [ "$rc" -eq 1 ] || { echo "expected exit 1, got $rc"; exit 1; }; python3 -c "import json
d=json.load(open('$OUT/preflight.json'))
bf=d.get('blocking_findings', [])
assert any('preflight_verdict.schema.json' in b for b in bf), bf
assert any('ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' in b for b in bf), bf
" && echo PASS || { echo "missing-reference FAIL did not name both missing paths"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: UNIT/BEHAVIOR FIXTURE (PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS, pending): a disposable git fixture with a declared-tolerated untracked file and NO operator-approval flags yields exit 2, withholds the schema-gated preflight.json entirely, and its informational preflight.pending.json correctly FAILS schema validation (no smuggled, unapproved PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS artifact).

UNIT/BEHAVIOR FIXTURE (PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS, approved) + CONTENT HONESTY (per .agent/qa/041-contract-review.json HIGH finding): the same fixture re-invoked with --operator-approver/--operator-authorization yields exit 3, both artifacts schema-valid, operator_approval fields exactly matching the supplied flags (not a placeholder), AND the baseline's repository_references/framework_references revision values equal the fixture's independently-recomputed real `git rev-parse HEAD`, its captured_at timestamp is fresh, and its ci_references item carries a genuine honest limitation note with no fabricated run_id/verified-green claim.
verification.command:
T=$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p "$T/schema"; cp schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json "$T/schema/"; ( cd "$T" && git init -q && git config user.email a@a.com && git config user.name a && git add -A && git commit -q -m init ); echo x > "$T/NOTES.md"; REAL_HEAD=$(git -C "$T" rev-parse HEAD); OUT=$(mktemp -d); python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id verify-approved --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$T" --tolerate-untracked NOTES.md --operator-approver jane --operator-authorization 'reviewed exceptions, approved'; rc=$?; [ "$rc" -eq 3 ] || { echo "expected exit 3, got $rc"; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
import json, jsonschema, datetime
pv = json.load(open('schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json'))
bv = json.load(open('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json'))
d = json.load(open('$OUT/preflight.json'))
jsonschema.validate(d, pv)
assert d['operator_approval']['approver'] == 'jane', d
assert d['operator_approval']['authorization'] == 'reviewed exceptions, approved', d
b = json.load(open('$OUT/baseline.json'))
jsonschema.validate(b, bv)
real_head = '$REAL_HEAD'
assert b['repository_references'][0]['revision'] == real_head, ('repository_references revision mismatch', b['repository_references'][0]['revision'], real_head)
assert b['framework_references'][0]['revision'] == real_head, ('framework_references revision mismatch', b['framework_references'][0]['revision'], real_head)
ci = b['ci_references'][0]
note = ci.get('note', '')
assert isinstance(note, str) and note.strip(), ('ci_references missing a non-empty honest limitation note', ci)
assert 'ci' in note.lower() and ('limitation' in note.lower() or 'no' in note.lower() or 'not' in note.lower()), ('ci_references note does not read as an honest limitation statement', ci)
assert 'run_id' not in ci, ('ci_references fabricates a run_id as if a real CI run were checked', ci)
assert ci.get('status') != 'verified-green', ('ci_references fabricates a verified-green claim', ci)
ts = b['captured_at'].rstrip('Z')
parsed = datetime.datetime.fromisoformat(ts).replace(tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)
now = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc)
delta = abs((now - parsed).total_seconds())
assert delta < 600, ('captured_at is stale or not close to the actual run clock', b['captured_at'], delta)
" && echo PASS || { echo "approved-exceptions fixture did not behave as contracted"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: UNIT/BEHAVIOR FIXTURE (clean PASS) + CONTENT HONESTY (per .agent/qa/041-contract-review.json HIGH finding): a fully clean disposable git fixture yields exit 0, both artifacts schema-valid, neither an 'exceptions' nor an 'operator_approval' key is smuggled into a plain PASS verdict, AND the baseline's repository_references/framework_references revision values equal the fixture's independently-recomputed real `git rev-parse HEAD`, its captured_at timestamp is fresh, and its ci_references item carries a genuine honest limitation note with no fabricated run_id/verified-green claim.
verification.command:
T=$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p "$T/schema"; cp schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json "$T/schema/"; ( cd "$T" && git init -q && git config user.email a@a.com && git config user.name a && git add -A && git commit -q -m init ); REAL_HEAD=$(git -C "$T" rev-parse HEAD); OUT=$(mktemp -d); python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id verify-clean --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$T"; rc=$?; [ "$rc" -eq 0 ] || { echo "expected exit 0, got $rc"; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
import json, jsonschema, datetime
pv = json.load(open('schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json'))
bv = json.load(open('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json'))
d = json.load(open('$OUT/preflight.json'))
jsonschema.validate(d, pv)
assert d['verdict'] == 'PASS', d
assert 'exceptions' not in d, d
assert 'operator_approval' not in d, d
b = json.load(open('$OUT/baseline.json'))
jsonschema.validate(b, bv)
real_head = '$REAL_HEAD'
assert b['repository_references'][0]['revision'] == real_head, ('repository_references revision mismatch', b['repository_references'][0]['revision'], real_head)
assert b['framework_references'][0]['revision'] == real_head, ('framework_references revision mismatch', b['framework_references'][0]['revision'], real_head)
ci = b['ci_references'][0]
note = ci.get('note', '')
assert isinstance(note, str) and note.strip(), ('ci_references missing a non-empty honest limitation note', ci)
assert 'ci' in note.lower() and ('limitation' in note.lower() or 'no' in note.lower() or 'not' in note.lower()), ('ci_references note does not read as an honest limitation statement', ci)
assert 'run_id' not in ci, ('ci_references fabricates a run_id as if a real CI run were checked', ci)
assert ci.get('status') != 'verified-green', ('ci_references fabricates a verified-green claim', ci)
ts = b['captured_at'].rstrip('Z')
parsed = datetime.datetime.fromisoformat(ts).replace(tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)
now = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc)
delta = abs((now - parsed).total_seconds())
assert delta < 600, ('captured_at is stale or not close to the actual run clock', b['captured_at'], delta)
" && echo PASS || { echo "clean PASS fixture did not behave as contracted"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: DETERMINISM (protocol Sec 2: 'running it twice against the same unchanged state MUST produce the same verdict'): two runs against the same unchanged clean fixture, different execution-ids, produce the identical PASS verdict both times.
verification.command: T=$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p "$T/schema"; cp schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json "$T/schema/"; ( cd "$T" && git init -q && git config user.email a@a.com && git config user.name a && git add -A && git commit -q -m init ); O1=$(mktemp -d); O2=$(mktemp -d); python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id verify-rep-1 --output-dir "$O1" --repo-root "$T" >/dev/null; python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id verify-rep-2 --output-dir "$O2" --repo-root "$T" >/dev/null; V1=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('$O1/preflight.json'))['verdict'])"); V2=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('$O2/preflight.json'))['verdict'])"); [ "$V1" = "$V2" ] && [ "$V1" = "PASS" ] && echo PASS || { echo "verdict not repeatable across two runs: $V1 vs $V2"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: USAGE ERROR: invoking the CLI with a missing required argument exits exactly 64, distinct from every domain exit code (0/1/2/3).
verification.command: python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --output-dir /tmp/does-not-matter >/dev/null 2>&1; rc=$?; [ "$rc" -eq 64 ] && echo PASS || { echo "expected exit 64 on a missing required argument, got $rc"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: BLOCKING-PRECEDENCE (protocol Sec 4 non-downgrade invariant, adversarial probe): a fixture combining a dirty tracked file, a declared-tolerated untracked file, AND supplied operator-approval flags simultaneously still returns FAIL/exit 1 -- a blocking condition is never overridden by exceptions or an operator's approval.
verification.command: T=$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p "$T/schema"; cp schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json "$T/schema/"; ( cd "$T" && git init -q && git config user.email a@a.com && git config user.name a && git add -A && git commit -q -m init ); echo x > "$T/NOTES.md"; echo '// dirty' >> "$T/schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json"; OUT=$(mktemp -d); python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id verify-precedence --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$T" --tolerate-untracked NOTES.md --operator-approver x --operator-authorization y; rc=$?; [ "$rc" -eq 1 ] && echo PASS || { echo "expected FAIL (exit 1) to win over exceptions+approval, got $rc"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: .agent/ immutability guard (two-part invariant, per 038 amendment_2 / 040 precedent, glob range covering the current phase-005 in-flight window 034-042): (a) no TRACKED/HISTORICAL .agent/qa or .agent/contracts record was modified; (b) every UNTRACKED addition under .agent/qa or .agent/contracts is confined to a legitimately in-flight phase-005 feature (034-042) or its review artifacts.
verification.command: bad=0; if ! git diff --quiet HEAD -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts; then echo "tracked/historical .agent/qa or .agent/contracts record modified:"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts; bad=1; fi; while IFS= read -r line; do case "$line" in "?? "*) path="${line:3}"; case "$path" in .agent/contracts/03[4-9]*.json|.agent/contracts/04[0-2]*.json|.agent/qa/03[4-9]*.json|.agent/qa/04[0-2]*.json) ;; *) echo "out-of-scope untracked .agent/qa or .agent/contracts artifact: $path"; bad=1 ;; esac ;; esac; done < <(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts); if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: Scope guard (untracked-aware, concurrency-tolerant): only this feature's own new file, plus the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md (tolerated, not authorized to be touched), changed outside .agent/.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'tools/ecosystem_preflight.py' 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected"; exit 1; }

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/041-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/041-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/041-verify-22.txt for the twenty-second -- the 'verification' array has exactly 22 entries). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.

approvals.round0_disposition: REJECTED (single HIGH finding: entries 5/11/12 validated baseline.json's shape against schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json but never its truth against real repository state -- a mutant CLI with fully fabricated baseline content (unrelated hardcoded SHA, stale hardcoded timestamp, a false 'verified-green' CI claim in place of the promised honest limitation note) passed all 22 entries unmodified, contradicting design_notes' real-HEAD-anchoring and honest-ci-note claims. FIX in this revision: entries 5, 11, and 12 each gained three independently-recomputed content-honesty assertions layered after their existing schema-validation step -- exact `git rev-parse HEAD` match for repository_references/framework_references, a genuine non-empty honest ci_references note with no fabricated run_id/verified-green claim, and a freshness bound on captured_at. Re-proven in both directions on the disposable clone: the honest prototype passes entries 5/11/12 with the new assertions active (22/22 overall, unchanged from round 0 on the other 19 entries); a freshly rebuilt scratch mutant reproducing the evaluator's exact fabrication shape fails all three revised entries with assertion messages naming the exact SHA/timestamp/CI-claim mismatch. status remains 'proposed'; scope, non_goals, the other 19 verification entries, and estimated_lines (260) are unchanged by this revision -- only entries 5/11/12's command and acceptance_test text, plus design_notes, were revised.
